note: Manually compiled policy-action list, LOCKED before any congressional buy-cluster matching (adding events after seeing buy-cluster dates is a pre-registration violation). 'Sector-favorable' = direction:'favorable'. favorable/adverse is the effect on the named sector_bucket (e.g. steel/aluminum tariffs are FAVORABLE to domestic steel producers = SLX; China tariffs are ADVERSE to KWEB). EO-anchored entries cite the real Executive Order number/date present in the V1 federal_register catalog (research/political_alpha/data/trump_events.jsonl); a few well-known major actions are included with best-known effective dates and flagged confidence:'medium'. Direction is a judgment call per spec §10 — rationale in each notes field.

events.description: EO 14148 + 'Unleashing American Energy' / National Energy Emergency — rescind climate rules, expand drilling/leasing
events.notes: Pro-fossil-fuel deregulation + leasing expansion is favorable to US energy producers (XLE). EO-anchored (PA-0001).
General deregulation posture favorable to financials (XLF). EO-anchored (PA-0005). Medium confidence on sector effect.

events.description: EO 14193 — 25% tariffs on Canada/Mexico imports, 10% on China (IEEPA)
events.notes: Import tariffs are protective for domestic steel/metals producers (SLX favorable). Same action is adverse to KWEB (see PE-004). EO-anchored (PA-0022).

events.description: EO 14193 — 10% additional tariff on Chinese imports
events.notes: China tariffs are adverse to China-tech (KWEB). Recorded for completeness; the Track B test uses favorable events only.

events.description: Section 232 steel & aluminum tariffs (25%) take effect, exemptions removed
events.notes: Classic protective tariff — favorable to domestic steel/aluminum (SLX). Well-known action; effective date used.
EO-anchored (PA-0073). V1 tag 'gold'; classified as energy/deregulation-adjacent favorable — medium confidence.

events.description: EO 14257 — 'Liberation Day' reciprocal tariffs (10% baseline on all imports + country add-ons)
events.notes: Broad protective tariff wall — favorable to domestic metals (SLX). Broadly adverse to import-dependent industrials (see PE-008). EO-anchored (PA-0086/PA-0087).

events.description: EO 14257 — reciprocal tariffs raise input costs for import-dependent industrials
events.notes: Supply-chain cost shock is adverse to industrials (XLI) and China-tech (KWEB). Recorded for completeness.

events.description: 90-day pause on reciprocal tariffs for non-retaliating countries (China excluded)
events.notes: Tariff relief is favorable to industrials (XLI). Well-known action; announcement date used.
EO-anchored (PA-0118). Critical-minerals/trade action favorable to metals/energy — medium confidence.

events.description: US-China Geneva agreement — mutual tariff reduction / 90-day truce
events.notes: Tariff de-escalation is favorable to China-tech (KWEB). Well-known action; announcement date used.
EO-anchored (PA-0154). Classified energy-favorable — medium confidence on sector effect.
EO-anchored (PA-0186). Regulatory-relief posture favorable to financials — medium confidence.

events.description: Section 232 steel/aluminum tariff rate doubled to 50%
events.notes: Tariff increase further protects domestic steel (SLX favorable). Well-known action; effective date used.
EO-anchored (PA-0201). China-tech action, classified adverse — medium confidence.
EO-anchored (PA-0250). Low confidence on rates/gold effect; included for completeness.
EO-anchored (PA-0268). Low-confidence industrial classification; included for completeness.
EO-anchored (PA-0314). Steel-tagged in V1; protective classification favorable to SLX — medium confidence.
EO-anchored (PA-0329). Energy-tagged in V1; pro-producer classification favorable to XLE — medium confidence.
EO-anchored (PA-0370). China-tech action classified adverse — medium confidence. Recorded for completeness.
EO-anchored (PA-0381). Energy-tagged favorable to XLE — medium confidence.
